<!--Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster--><!--Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster--><!--Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster--><!--Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster--><!--Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster--><!--Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[en:Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster]] {{wikidata link|Q18337610}} [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:History of Nakagawa Town]] [[Category:History of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:kk
}}
[[en:Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:Nakagawa, Tochigi]] [[Category:National historic sites in Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:pages]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster]][[ja:那須小川古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster]][[ja:那須小川古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster]][[ja:那須小川古墳群]]
{{Short description| part_ofs of Kofuns in Nakagawa, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster | native_name =那須小川古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|45|41|N|140|07|16|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en|Nakagawa, Tochigi|WD=Q44603|lt=Nakagawa, Tochigi|1=Nakagawa, Tochigi}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} | type = [[kofun]] groups | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster'''|那須小川古墳群 |}} is the collective designation for three separate groups of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the town of {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en|Nakagawa, Tochigi|WD=Q44603|lt=Nakagawa|1=Nakagawa, Tochigi}} in {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}} in the northern {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun within this cluster received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1979, and the other groups were added in 2002.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/160768/5|title=那須小川古墳群|trans-title= Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster were built near the Gozu River, a tributary of the [[Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Naka River]] in the Nasu region of northern Tochigi during the 4th century. The sites are located about 1 hour and 30 minutes by car from JR {{ill|Tōhoku Shinkansen|en|Tōhoku Shinkansen|WD=Q900665|lt=Tōhoku Shinkansen|1=Tōhoku Shinkansen}} {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|en|Utsunomiya Station|WD=Q801549|lt=Utsunomiya Station|1=Utsunomiya Station}}.The cluster consists of the following:
==={{nihongo|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|駒形大塚古墳}}=== This keyhole-shaped ''kofun'' has a total length of 64 meters. The circular portion has a diameter of 16 meters. The rectangular portion has a length of 32 meters, with of 30 meters and height of 8 meters. It was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. The tumulus was excavated in 1974, and was found to contain a [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a wooden casket with length of 3.2 meters and width of 75 centimeters. Many {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} were recovered, including earthenware, [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, two knives, an iron ax, sword, as well as copper and glass ornaments. The ''kofun'' became a nationally designated historic site on March 13, 1979 and is currently maintained as Komagata Park. {{coord|36|45|42|N|140|7|14|E|region:JP-09_scale:20000}}
==={{nihongo|Yoshida Onsen Jinja Kofun group|吉田温泉神社古墳群}}=== This group of tumuli consisted of one cojoined-square ''kofun'' (named the Yoshida Onsen Jinja Kofun) and twenty square ''kofun'', of which only four have survived. The Yoshida Onsen Jinja Kofun was built around the end of the 4th century, the early Kofun period, but the rear part has been cut away. A {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} was built on the remaining mound, thus accounting for the name of this tumulus. Based on several surveys, the tumulus has a total length of 47 meters. The rear portion measured 25 meters by 21 meters, and the width of the portion joining the two mounds was 10 meters. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat. Grave goods included earthenware, an iron sword, and an iron ax, but no ''fukiishi'' were detected. The foundations of a [[:en:Pit dwelling|Pit dwelling]] measuring eight meters in diameter, with an altar-like step about 25 cm in height on the east side wall, was found, indicating that this was a ceremonial structure. {{coord|36|45|00.0|N|140|8|2.5|E|region:JP-09|display=}}
==={{nihongo|Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun group|那須八幡塚古墳群}}=== This group consists of two tumuli. The '''Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun''' is a cojoined-square ''kofun'' with a length of 62 meters. It was built on a [[:en:river terrace|river terrace]] on the west bank of the [[Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Naka River]]. They rear portion has width of 31 meters and height of 6 meters, and the front portion has a width of 17 meters. The tumulus was covered in ''fukiishi'', and was surrounded by a mounded terrace and irregular moat. In an excavation conducted in 1952, the burial chamber with a split bamboo-shaped wooden casket with clay blocks on both ends in the east and west was confirmed one meter below the summit of the rear portion. Grave goods included bronze mirrors, iron swords, iron ax, knives, and sickle. The walls of the burial chamber were covered with vermilion. It is believed that this ''kofun'' was built in the latter half of the 4th century from the earthenware discovered, and it is considered to postdate the Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun and Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun.
The {{nihongo|'''Yoshida Fujiyama Kofun'''|吉田富士山古墳}} is a trapezoidal ''kofun'' measuring 7 meters on each side except for the south, which is 8.5 meters. The tumulus is 8.5 meters high. Two pit dwellings older than the Kofun have been excavated nearby. {{coord|36|44|43.3|N|140|08|09.5|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark}}
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tochigi)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== * [http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/life/shisetsu/nasuogawakohungun.html Nakagawa town home page] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}
{{draft categories| <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[ja:那須小川古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|part_of={{ill|Komagata Otsuka Kofun|WD=Q11668363|1=Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun}}}} '''Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster''' (那須小川古墳群) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun group|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en|Nakagawa, Tochigi|WD=Q44603|lt=Nakagawa, Tochigi|1=Nakagawa, Tochigi}}, Tochigi Prefecture. It comprises the {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|駒形大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group, and Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group. On December 19, 2002, it was designated a National Historic Site<ref>[http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp Cultural Properties Database (Nationally Designated)]</ref>.
== Overview ==
This kofun group, built during the 4th century along the Gonzu River basin, consists of three zenpō-kōhō-fun (keyhole-shaped Kofuns): {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|駒形大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun, and Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun, along with 21 square mounds<ref name=ogawa>[http://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/23syougai/03bunka/nasuogawakohungun.html Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster - Nakagawa Town Official Site]</ref>. Numerous grave goods were unearthed, including a Chinese-made mirror called the "Four-Beast Mirror with Decorative Band" from Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun and the "Kui Feng Mirror" from Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun. Evidence of related funeral structures, such as a temporary mortuary or management facilities, was discovered near Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun<ref name=ogawa />.
Below are descriptions of the main Kofuns within this group.
== Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun ==
{{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|駒形大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} is a zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Ogawa district. While the front section is partially leveled, the rear section remains largely intact. The mound measures 64 meters in length and yielded various grave goods, including a Chinese-imported "Four-Beast Mirror with Decorative Band." It was constructed around the mid-4th century. {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|name=Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun}} {{Main|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun}}
== Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group ==
This group consists of the Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun (1st mound) and 20 square mounds (Yoshida Onsen Shrine Mounds 2–21). Only five mounds (Mounds 1–3 and 20–21, also called Kannondō Kofun) have preserved mounds<ref name=ogawamachi>Ogawa Town Board of Education, "National Historic Site: Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster," April 2003, pp. 12–31.</ref>.
=== Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun ===
Located in the Ogawa district, this zenpō-kōhō-fun measures 47 meters in length. The rear section has been leveled, and the Onsen Shrine stands on the summit of the front section. Nearby, a pit structure with offering pottery was discovered, believed to have served as a temporary mortuary for funeral ceremonies. Constructed around the late 4th century. {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|name=Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun}} {{Main|Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun}}
=== Kannondō Kofun ===
A square mound with one side measuring 30 meters. Although approximately a quarter of the mound has been lost to river flooding, it features a surrounding ditch with a maximum width of 9 meters in the south and 6 meters in the northwest. Excavations in 1981 and 1991 uncovered haniwa pedestals and decorative jars. {{Coord|36|44|52.80|N|140|8|4.87|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|name=Kannondō Kofun}}
== Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group ==
This group comprises two mounds: Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun (1st mound) and Yoshida Fujiyama Kofun (2nd mound).
=== Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun ===
A zenpō-kōhō-fun located in the Yoshida district, measuring 62 meters in length. Various grave goods, including an imported Kui Feng Mirror, were unearthed here. Constructed in the late 4th century, it has been restored and is open to the public. {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|name=Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun}} {{Main|Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun}}
=== Yoshida Fujiyama Kofun ===
A square mound with a side length of 7 meters and a height of 3 meters, surrounded by a ditch with a maximum width of 8.5 meters to the south. Two older pit structures were discovered outside the surrounding ditch during excavations. {{Coord|36|44|37.90|N|140|8|11.42|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|name=Yoshida Fujiyama Kofun}}

== Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun ==
{{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|駒形大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} is a zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Ogawa district. While the front section is partially leveled, the rear section remains largely intact. The mound measures 64 meters in length and yielded various grave goods, including a Chinese-imported "Four-Beast Mirror with Decorative Band." It was constructed around the mid-4th century. {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|name=Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun}} {{Main|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun}}
== Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun Group ==
This group consists of the Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun (1st mound) and 20 square mounds (Yoshida Onsen Shrine Mounds 2–21). Only five mounds (Mounds 1–3 and 20–21, also called Kannondō Kofun) have preserved mounds<ref name=ogawamachi>Ogawa Town Board of Education, "National Historic Site: Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster," April 2003, pp. 12–31.</ref>.
=== Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun ===
Located in the Ogawa district, this zenpō-kōhō-fun measures 47 meters in length. The rear section has been leveled, and the Onsen Shrine stands on the summit of the front section. Nearby, a pit structure with offering pottery was discovered, believed to have served as a temporary mortuary for funeral ceremonies. Constructed around the late 4th century. {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|name=Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun}} {{Main|Yoshida Onsen Shrine Kofun}}
=== Kannondō Kofun ===
A square mound with one side measuring 30 meters. Although approximately a quarter of the mound has been lost to river flooding, it features a surrounding ditch with a maximum width of 9 meters in the south and 6 meters in the northwest. Excavations in 1981 and 1991 uncovered haniwa pedestals and decorative jars. {{Coord|36|44|52.80|N|140|8|4.87|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|name=Kannondō Kofun}}
== Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun Group ==
This group comprises two mounds: Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun (1st mound) and Yoshida Fujiyama Kofun (2nd mound).
=== Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun ===
A zenpō-kōhō-fun located in the Yoshida district, measuring 62 meters in length. Various grave goods, including an imported Kui Feng Mirror, were unearthed here. Constructed in the late 4th century, it has been restored and is open to the public. {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|name=Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun}} {{Main|Nasu Hachimanzuka Kofun}}
=== Yoshida Fujiyama Kofun ===
A square mound with a side length of 7 meters and a height of 3 meters, surrounded by a ditch with a maximum width of 8.5 meters to the south. Two older pit structures were discovered outside the surrounding ditch during excavations. {{Coord|36|44|37.90|N|140|8|11.42|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|name=Yoshida Fujiyama Kofun}}
